Functions and types for managing and displaying timeline action menus in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions and types for managing and interacting with timeline actions within an application. It includes the definition of an enumeration `TimelineItemActionSource`, which specifies different sources from which a timeline item action can originate, such as notifications or user interfaces. The code provides functions to create and manage action menus, which are used to display and handle user actions related to timeline items. These functions include [`timeline_actions_add_action_to_root_level`](<#timeline_actions_add_action_to_root_level>), which adds an action to a specified menu level, and [`timeline_actions_create_action_menu_root_level`](<#timeline_actions_create_action_menu_root_level>), which initializes the root level of an action menu with a specified number of actions.

Additionally, the code includes functions to display action menus on the screen, such as [`timeline_actions_push_action_menu`](<#timeline_actions_push_action_menu>) and [`timeline_actions_push_response_menu`](<#timeline_actions_push_response_menu>), which create and push action menus to the user interface. The function [`timeline_actions_dismiss_all`](<#timeline_actions_dismiss_all>) is used to dismiss all notifications associated with a given list, and [`timeline_actions_invoke_action`](<#timeline_actions_invoke_action>) is responsible for executing a specified timeline action. The code is intended to be part of a larger application, as indicated by the inclusion of headers and the use of types and functions that are likely defined elsewhere.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/action_menu_window_private.h`
- `services/normal/notifications/notification_types.h`
- `services/normal/timeline/item.h`


# Data Structures

---
### TimelineItemActionSource
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - `TimelineItemActionSourceModalNotification`: Represents an action source from a modal notification.
    - `TimelineItemActionSourceNotificationApp`: Represents an action source from a notification application.
    - `TimelineItemActionSourceTimeline`: Represents an action source from the timeline.
    - `TimelineItemActionSourceSendTextApp`: Represents an action source from a send text application.
    - `TimelineItemActionSourcePhoneUi`: Represents an action source from the phone UI.
- **Description**: Defines different sources from which a timeline item action can originate, such as modal notifications, notification apps, timeline, send text apps, and phone UI.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### timeline\_actions\_create\_action\_menu\_root\_level<!-- {{#callable_declaration:timeline_actions_create_action_menu_root_level}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/timeline/timeline_actions.h#L41>)

Creates the root level for a Timeline ActionMenu.
- **Description**: Use this function to create the root level of a Timeline ActionMenu, which is necessary for managing timeline actions. This function must be called with the number of actions the root level will hold, a separator index, and the source of the timeline item action. It sets the current timeline item action source and requests a responsive session. Ensure that the number of actions and separator index are within valid ranges for the application.
- **Inputs**:
    - `num_actions`: The number of actions the root level will hold. Must be a non-negative integer within the range of a `uint8_t`.
    - `separator_index`: The index at which a separator is placed in the action menu. Must be a non-negative integer within the range of a `uint8_t`.
    - `source`: The source of the timeline item action, specified as a `TimelineItemActionSource` enum value. This parameter determines the context in which the action menu is created.
- **Output**: A pointer to the created `ActionMenuLevel`, representing the root level of the action menu.
- **See Also**: [`timeline_actions_create_action_menu_root_level`](<timeline_actions.c.md#timeline_actions_create_action_menu_root_level>)  (Implementation)


---
### timeline\_actions\_push\_response\_menu<!-- {{#callable_declaration:timeline_actions_push_response_menu}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/timeline/timeline_actions.h#L61>)

Creates and displays a response action menu for a timeline item.
- **Description**: Use this function to create and display a response action menu associated with a specific timeline item. This menu allows users to interact with the timeline item through a set of predefined actions. The function requires a timeline item and an action to be added to the menu. It also allows customization of the menu's background color and provides a callback for when the menu is closed. The function must be called with a valid window stack to display the menu. The `standalone_reply` parameter modifies the menu text to provide context when the menu is displayed independently of other menus.
- **Inputs**:
    - `item`: A pointer to the `TimelineItem` associated with this menu. Must not be null.
    - `reply_action`: A pointer to the `TimelineItemAction` to add to the menu. Must not be null.
    - `bg_color`: The background color of the action menu. Must be a valid `GColor` value.
    - `did_close_cb`: A callback function that is called when the action menu is closed. Can be null if no callback is needed.
    - `window_stack`: A pointer to the `WindowStack` where the action menu will be pushed. Must not be null.
    - `source`: The source of the action menu, indicating which window or app pushed it. Must be a valid `TimelineItemActionSource` value.
    - `standalone_reply`: A boolean value that, when true, changes the "Voice" text to "Reply with Voice" to provide better context when the menu is displayed without a preceding menu.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the created `ActionMenu`. The caller is responsible for managing the lifecycle of the returned `ActionMenu`.
- **See Also**: [`timeline_actions_push_response_menu`](<timeline_actions.c.md#timeline_actions_push_response_menu>)  (Implementation)
